_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Chemical burns: Without significant facial scarring and/or loss of function; Includes % TBSA skin grafted, flap cover, follow-up dressings etc. as deemed necessary; Surgical procedures are required for deep burns that are not amenable to heal with dressings alone.
min_doctor_qualification: M. Ch. / DNB / or equivalent (in Plastic Surgery)
clinical_indications: Surgical procedures are required for deep burns that are not amenable to heal with dressings alone.

clinical_thresholds.value: deep partial thickness or full thickness

preauth.label: Clinical notes mentioning the circumstances that led to Chemical Burn
Clinical Photograph of the affected part

claim.label: Document showing % of burn through rule of 9
Blood test (CBC, Sr. creatinine, Platelet etc.)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes clearly mentioning the areas affected by burn with % of TBSA burn as per rule of 9?
Was the Copy of MLC with number submitted?
Clinical photograph of the affected part
Did the Clinical notes mentioning the circumstances that led to Chemical burn submitted?
Has the patient presented with either all or some burns of deep partial thickness or full thickness?

cpd_claim.q: Is there documentary evidence of indication of surgery submitted?
Are detailed operative notes available with indications for surgery and outcomes of the procedure?
Is discharge summary available with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Is the post treatment photograph available?
Was all the lab test report submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes for chemical burn circumstances missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
MLC copy with number missing at pre-authorization.
Clinical photograph of affected area not submitted at pre-authorization.
Documentation for % TBSA burn (Rule of 9) missing at claim submission.
Patient's burn depth not documented as deep partial thickness or full thickness, or documentation is insufficient.
Operative notes lacking details on indications for surgery or procedure outcomes at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Discoloration of the skin with clear line of demarcation
Brown or black with sulphuric acid and yellow with nitric acid
The trickle marks are the special features
Chemical substance may be present over the surface and the clothing's
The acid burn victim suffers from severe pain and burning
